Events

The Library is here to help you learn and connect with your community through our wide array of free events, programs, classes, book clubs, and more. Please check listings to confirm if a program is in-person, online, or outdoors.

If you have any questions about the events listed below, please contact the branch for more information. See here for service updates at the Library.

Discover our wide array of free online events.

Borough:

Reading & Literary Culture
Dante's Paradiso: Eleven Weeks of Close Reading and Discussion
REGISTRATION IS CLOSED - We are full! For more information, email Corinne at corinneneary@nypl.org. This is a continuation of our Divine Comedy discussion, led by Frank and Corinne. Mondays 1-2:30pm in our third floor Mae West room. We are using the Mary Jo Bang translation of Paradiso. "Mary Jo Bang's translation of Paradiso completes her groundbreaking new version of Dante's masterpiece, begun with Inferno and continued with Purgatorio. In Paradiso, Dante has been purified by his…

Today
@ 1 PM
STEAM
Take Home Kit: A Message To The Future
What can we leave for future New Yorkers to tell them about life in 2026? Some items buried in New York were chosen on purpose, such as time capsules at the World’s Fair in Queens. Others were left behind by chance, such as items from Seneca Village near Central Park. This program is first come first served, while supplies last. ​​ Major support provided by the New York Life Foundation, the 2026 city-wide sponsor for the Summer Reading and Learning Programs. Additional support is…

Today
@ 1 PM
Online
Online Book Discussion
Join us for a virtual book discussion. Have a book you've read recently and want to talk about? Email ahead of time and we'll make sure you have time to share with the group. Just want to listen to some book recommendations? That's great too, we'll share what we've been reading. This will happen through 'Google Meet'. If you'd like to participate or listen in, register here providing your email address for a link.

@ 1 PM
Resume Help: One-on-One Session
Looking for a new job? The library can help! Get help writing or revising your resume, and learn how to use websites like Indeed and Google to find current openings.
Phone or in-person		125th Street LibraryJob Seekers,

Persons Without Homes
Today
@ 1 PM
Fiber Arts
Are you a knitter? Do you like to crochet? Maybe you like a different type of fiber art? Whichever it is, bring your project and supplies and lose yourself in the meditative world of creating art with fiber. Invite your friends, or make new connections here. This is not a class.

Today
@ 1 PM		Canceled
TechConnect
*In-Person: Advanced Python Functions Part 2: Closures and Decorators
This event will take place in person at the Stavros Niarchos Foundation Library in the Mac Lab (617). Continuing from Advanced Python Functions Part 1, this class dives deeper into higher-order function concepts with a focus on closures and decorators. You’ll learn how to create functions that "remember" state and apply decorators to extend functionality in clean, reusable ways. (Prerequisite: Advanced Python Functions Part 1) This class requires a gmail account to use Google Colab, a b…
Online registration is now closed		Stavros Niarchos Foundation Library (SNFL), Pasculano Learning Center
Mac Lab		Adults
Today
@ 1:30 PM
Yorkville Book Discussion Group
Join us for book discussion group at Yorkville! This is a hybrid program that includes a Zoom component. We will be reading Long Island Compromise by Taffy Brodesser-Akner (444pgs). Fletcher is kidnapped from his driveway, the ransom is paid and the family moves on. The family fortune dwindled and the now grown 3 children aren’t doing much better than their parents. A brew of hilarity and heartbreak that recalls Philip Roth’s novels of strong family ties. Readers are responsible for acquirin…
Yorkville Library, Meeting RoomAdults
Today
@ 1:30 PM
Online
*Online: Intro to Adobe Photoshop
This event will take place online via Zoom. Must register with a valid email address. Learn how to enhance photos using the # 1 photo editing software in the world: Adobe Photoshop. Get a basic introduction to Photoshop's tools, layers, and adjustments as you work through two example projects. This class is part of our Design 360 class series. To learn more, visit: bit.ly/43wQ2eq - You will receive an email 2 hours prior to the start of the class with the link to join the virtual class. *P…

@ 2 PM
US 250
Spirit of '76 Creative Writing
Hone your creative writing skills and get helpful feedback from other writers! This program series will be held in person at the Battery Park City Library. Join us for this engaging three-part writing workshop reflecting on the 250th anniversary of the American Revolution. These sessions are co-presented by the Battery Park City Authority and led by author and poet Jon Curley. Attendees will be encouraged to use reflection as a way to enhance their writing styles in any preferred mode. Beginner…

Today
@ 2 PM
TechConnect
*In-Person: Mac Basics
This event will take place in person at the Countee Cullen Library in the TechConnect Lab. This introductory class will teach you the fundamental skills needed to navigate your Mac computer with confidence, from using the mouse to finding your saved files. You will learn how to open your favorite programs, stay organized, and understand the basic features that make using an Apple computer easy and enjoyable.
Online registration is now closed		Countee Cullen Library, TechConnect Computer LabAdults

TechConnect
Computer Basics: Open Lab
This event will take place in person at George Bruce Library. Do you have technology questions? Drop by the library between 2 and 3 p.m. No appointments needed -- first come, first served! @ 2 PM
Comics Creativity
Come join us to be creative, explore various aspects of comics, and meet other comics-loving kids. Collaborate to make comics, posters, and buttons. Most importantly, have fun! This program is open to children ages 6-12 and their grown ups.

Today
@ 2 PM
Diamond Art Painting
This is an in-person event at the Fort Washington Library. Melt away stress while creating your own gorgeous Diamond Painting masterpiece. It involves placing tiny resin “diamond” or “drills” onto an adhesive, color-coded canvas to form a vibrant, sparkling picture. Materials will be provided. Registration Required…

@ 2 PM
Open Lab: Tech Help
If you have tech questions and want one-on-one assistance related to: Email Internet browsing Typing documents or using Word and Google Docs NYPL mobile apps (the NYPL app, Libby, Cloud Library) & databases General tech troubleshooting We can help! Bring your own device, or use a laptop provided by the library. No registration required.

@ 2 PM		Canceled
Gaming Hour
This event will take place in person at Macomb's Bridge Library. Like to play games? Come play with us here at the Bridge! Each week we'll feature different gaming books and games, ranging from board and card games to video games For children ages 5 - 12 years old. Registration is not required for this event.

Today
@ 2 PM
US 250
Monday Matinee: Mr. Smith Goes to Washington (1939)
Join us for a Monday Matinee: Celebrating 250 Years of the United States at the St. Agnes Library featuring classic and contemporary films. Mr. Smith Goes to Washington (Frank Capra, 1939) July 13, 2pm When the idealistic young Jefferson Smith winds up appointed to the United States Senate, he gains the mentorship of Senator Joseph Paine. However, Paine isn't as noble as his reputation would indicate, and he becomes involved in a scheme to discredit Smtih, who wants to build a boys' campsi…
St. Agnes LibraryAdults
Today
@ 3 PM
US 250
Daytime Documentary - Hamtramck, USA
This program will take place in person at Webster. Join us for a screening of Hamtramck, USA. Anchored in public arts and the mayoral election, this documentary explores multiculturalism in Hamtramck, Michigan and how it transforms the community and the residents who live there. Dir. Justin Feltman, Razi Jafri. 2020. 93 mins. via Projectr. Please note: All films are subject to last minute substitutions.
First come, first served		Webster Library, AuditoriumAdults
Today
@ 3 PM
Computer Basics: Open Lab
This event will take place in person at Harlem Library. Do you have technology questions? Drop by the library between 3pm - 4:30pm. No appointments needed, first come, first served! The following are examples of topics with which we can help: Resume and Cover Letter Review, Learn to type and use a mouse, Internet for Beginners, Email for Beginners Windows 10 Basics, Microsoft Word 2010 for Beginners, Connecting Devices to the Computer, Downloading NYPL E-Books & more.

@ 3 PM
Coloring Club
This event will take place in person at Hamilton Grange Library. Join us for a relaxing afternoon of coloring. Fun coloring pages will be provided, or simply get creative and color freely. All materials will be provided. Sign up for the biweekly NYPL Kids e-newsletter to get information on free children's programs, upcoming author talks, and more happening at the Library and online.

Teen Open Hours
This event will take place on the second floor of the Battery Park City Library. Teen Open Hours is from 3pm till 15 minutes before the branch closes, Monday through Friday. @ 3 PM
Summer at the Library
Summer Adventure: Harlem Renaissance Album Art
Join staff from the 125th Street Library for an exciting, hands-on program dedicated to the Harlem Renaissance! We will create our own album artwork inspired by art and music that came out of the Harlem Renaissance (1920s–1930s). The Harlem Renaissance influenced artists and musicians for decades after it peaked, including the ones whose works we will look at and listen to for this project Major support provided by the New York Life Foundation, the 2026 city-wide sponsor for the Summer Re…

@ 3 PM
Disability Pride Month
Sensory Storytime
This event will take place in person at the Andrew Heiskell Library. Join us for an inclusive and interactive storytime that encourages movement, promotes literacy, and offers safe sensory input. While open to everyone, this program is ideal for children with autism spectrum disorders, sensory sensitivities, or any child that may have difficulty attending in large groups. Registration is not required. Tickets will be available half an hour prior to the event. Space available for up to ten f…

@ 3 PM
Summer at the Library
Make Your Own Imaginary Creature: Needle Felting Workshop (Registration Closed)
Presented by Loop of the Loom. In this fiber workshop, children will explore the fun and magical world of needle felting while creating their own 3-dimensional animal or imaginary character. Using child-appropriate felting needles, young makers will learn to layer, poke, and shape loose wool into solid forms. Participants will work with a colorful assortment of natural wool and receive supportive guidance as they bring their ideas to life, taking home a small felted friend. This session sparks i…

@ 3:15 PM
Movie Monday - We're Back! A Dinosaur's Story (1993)
Join us for a screening of We're Back! A Dinosaur's Story (1993). A time traveler transports four amiable dinosaurs to modern New York. Cast: Rene LeVant, Walter Cronkite, Joey Shea, Ken Mars, Yeardley Smith Director(s): Ralph Zondag Rating: G Runtime: 1h 19min Open to all, no registration required. Seating is limited on a first-come, first-served basis.
